key_points.raw:
ABOUT [ edit ]

Indian Bank is a medium-sized bank which was started in 1907. It offers deposits, loans and services. The Bank's segments include Treasury, Corporate/Wholesale Banking, Retail Banking and Other Banking Operations. [1]

KEY POINTS [ edit ]

Market Position
Indian Bank is the seventh-largest public sector bank in terms of deposits and advances after the announcement of amalgamations for other PSBs. [1]

Revenue Mix Q3 FY26[2]
Retail Banking: ~41%
Corporate Banking: ~32%
Treasury: 24%
Others: 2%

Key Ratios
CRAR- ~16.58% in Q3 FY26 vs 16.53% in FY22 [3][4]
Domestic NIM - 3.4% in Q3 FY26 vs 2.9% in FY22[5]
Gross NPA - 2.23% in Q3 FY26 vs 8.47% in FY22
Net NPA - 0.15% in Q3 FY26 vs 2.27% in FY22
PCR - 98.28% in Q3 FY26 vs 87.38% in FY22 [6] [4]
The slippage ratio- 0.69% in Q3 FY26 vs 2.78% in FY22. [7][8]

Branch Network[9]
As of Q3 FY26, the bank has a network of ~5,965 domestic branches, ~5,624 ATMs, and ~16,247 business correspondents across India.
** Branch breakup:**
Rural: 34%
Semi-urban: 26%
Urban: 20%
Metro: 20%

Loan Book
As of Q3 FY26, total advances stood at Rs. ~6,39,000 Cr vs Rs. ~4,20,000 Cr in FY22 [6][4]

Agriculture: 25% in Q3 FY26 vs 22% in FY22
Retail: 23% in Q3 FY26 vs 20% in FY22
MSME: 18% in Q3 FY26 vs 19% in FY22
Corporates: 34% in Q3 FY26 vs 39% in FY22 [10][11]

Deposits
As of Q3 FY26, total deposits were Rs. 7,91,000 Cr vs Rs. ~ 5,90,000 Cr in FY22. [6][4]

CASA: 39.08% in Q3 FY26 vs 42.3% in FY22.
Term deposits: 62.6% in Q3 FY26 vs 51.7% in FY22. [12][13]

International Operations
The Bank has 3 foreign branches located in Singapore, Colombo, and Jaffna and 1 IFSC Banking Unit in Gujarat. [14] International business accounts for ~6% of the total business mix of the bank as of Q3 FY26.[12]

RRB Business[15]
The bank has 2 Regional Rural Bank (RRB) associates - Tamil Nadu Grama Bank & Paduvai Bharathiar Grama Bank. These combined possess ~733 branches with business of Rs.~60,305 Cr as of Q3 FY26.

Digital Advancement[16][17]
The bank continues to expand its digital footprint with digital business reaching ₹198,350 Cr in FY26 (NME), supported by strong growth across digital lending segments such as vehicle, home, jewel and MSME loans. As of Q3 FY26, digital adoption improved with mobile banking users at 2.25 Cr, transactions at 1.93 Cr, internet banking users at 1.18 Cr and debit card users at 3.52 Cr. UPI activity also increased with 2.52 Cr users and 210.53 Cr transactions, while merchant acceptance expanded with 24,125 PoS terminals and 27,044 FASTag users, reflecting continued growth in digital engagement.

Workforce[18]
As of Q3 FY26, the bank’s workforce stood at 40,515 employees with 31% female representation and an average age of 39 years. HR initiatives focused on employee development and inclusion through programs such as 3,226 training sessions (92,111 participations), AI training for 9,436 officers, Rashtriya Karmayogi training for 19,612 employees, and initiatives like Aparajitha (return-to-work for women), POSH training, and gamified micro-learning platforms to strengthen skills and workplace engagement.

GoI Shareholding
GoI’s ownership stands at ~74% as of Jun 2024 reduced from ~80% in Jun 2022. During Q3 FY24, the bank raised equity aggregating to Rs. ~4,000 Cr by QIP. [19] [20]

Outlook[21]
The Bank has set a target to double its business figure in five years, aiming for more than ₹25 lakh crore by Dec 2029.

KMP Change[22][23]
CRO & FRMD Mr. Sujit Kumar Dey retired effective 1st Jan 2026, and was replaced by Mr. Navin Kumar Shrivastava who has experience of more than 31 years in banking industry.
